name: bsd-mailx
title: bsd-mailx
description:
simple mail user agent
bsd-mailx is the traditional simple command-line-mode mail user agent.
Even if you don't use it, it may be required by other programs.
.
The /usr/bin/mail command provided by this package is a very simple one,
and does not include many features that can be found in more advanced
mail user agents.
.
For example bsd-mailx DOES NOT support:
- MIME           (i.e. no attachments, no UTF-8 or other charsets support);
- SMTP protocol  (/usr/sbin/sendmail interface only is used);
- POP3/IMAP      (bsd-mailx reads local mailboxes only);
- maildir format (traditional mbox only format is supported).
.
If the above features are needed, please consider installing another package
providing similar /usr/bin/mail interface:
- s-nail package,
- or mailutils package.

arguments.description: Specify additional header fields on the command line such as "X-Loop: foo@bar" etc.
arguments.raw: -a  Specify additional header fields on the command line such as "X-Loop: foo@bar" etc.

arguments.description: Causes mail to output all sorts of information useful for debugging mail.
arguments.raw: -d  Causes mail to output all sorts of information useful for debugging mail.

arguments.description: Don't send messages with an empty body.
arguments.raw: -E  Don't send messages with an empty body.

arguments.description: Use an alternate mailbox. Defaults to the user's mbox if no file is specified. When
arguments.raw: -f  Use an alternate mailbox. Defaults to the user's mbox if no file is specified. When

arguments.description: Forces mail to run in interactive mode, even when input is not a terminal. In partic-
arguments.raw: -I  Forces mail to run in interactive mode, even when input is not a terminal. In partic-

arguments.description: Ignore tty interrupt signals. This is particularly useful when using mail on noisy
arguments.raw: -i  Ignore tty interrupt signals. This is particularly useful when using mail on noisy

arguments.description: Verbose mode. The details of delivery are displayed on the user's terminal.
arguments.raw: -v  Verbose mode. The details of delivery are displayed on the user's terminal.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bsd-mailx because simple mail user agent.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use bsd-mailx as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bsd-mailx with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bsd-mailx --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
